skill.name: validate-requirements
skill.description: Checks whether user requirements are clear, complete, and aligned with saved project standards before work begins.
skill.summary: Validate requirements against project standards before starting a quality pipeline.
skill.author: AbeJitsu

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution and weak cryptography, but the cited lines are Markdown fences, prose, and JSON examples. No shell execution, network access, secret handling, or cryptographic code is present. The skill does instruct the assistant to read saved project standards, so publication is acceptable with a low filesystem-access note.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Fences Reported as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are fenced JavaScript and JSON examples, plus nearby Markdown text. They do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands and contain no user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visible Markdown code fencing or reference text, not executable Ruby or shell syntax. No command names, shell metacharacter construction, or execution instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detected in Plain Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts appear to match prose such as description or Describe, not cryptographic algorithms or APIs. No encryption, hashing, or credential-protection logic appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language requirements guidance and output examples. I found no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Bounded Project Standards File Access
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to load saved standards and references standards.json through a repository interface. This is legitimate for its purpose, but it relies on reading local project configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instructions clearly require reading saved project standards. The scope is narrow and local, so the risk is low rather than suspicious.

content.user_title: Validate Project Requirements Before Work Starts
content.value_statement: Unclear requirements cause wasted generation cycles and rework. This skill checks inputs against saved standards before a coding or content pipeline continues.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether user input is complete enough to begin work.
Compares requirements with saved project validation rules.
Identifies missing context, unclear goals, and conflicting requirements.
Returns a structured validation result with issues and recommendations.
Suggests specific clarification questions when input is incomplete.
limitations: Requires saved standards to apply project-specific validation rules.
Does not create or modify the final implementation.
Cannot verify external links or unavailable source material by itself.
Uses general validation when no project standards exist.

use_cases.title: Screen Component Requests
use_cases.description: Check whether a component request includes purpose, behavior, props, and constraints before generation starts.

use_cases.title: Validate Content Briefs
use_cases.description: Confirm that a writing prompt includes topic, audience, format, and success criteria before drafting begins.

use_cases.title: Gate Automation Pipelines
use_cases.description: Use a validation step before build, refactor, or documentation workflows continue.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic Requirements
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation before starting a task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate these requirements against the project standards. Tell me what is missing and what I should clarify before work begins: [paste requirements].

prompt_templates.title: Review a Feature Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review for product or engineering work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature brief for completeness, clarity, format, and standards alignment. Return the blocking issues first: [paste brief].

prompt_templates.title: Prepare Clarification Questions
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when requirements are close but incomplete.
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate this request and produce only the most important clarification questions needed to proceed safely: [paste request].

prompt_templates.title: Run Pipeline Gate Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced gate before a multi-step quality pipeline.
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as the first quality gate for this workflow. Load the relevant project standards, validate the input, and recommend whether to proceed or pause: [paste workflow input].

output: Missing purpose, expected options format, selection behavior, keyboard behavior, and required props.
Recommendation: ask for the usage context and interaction requirements before implementation.

output_examples.input: Create a searchable team member dropdown with keyboard navigation, options data, onSelect callback, and placeholder text.
output: The request includes purpose, expected behavior, key props, and accessibility-related interaction details.
Recommendation: proceed to the next quality step.

output_examples.input: Write a blog post about release planning.
output: The topic is present, but audience, format, length, tone, and success criteria are missing.
Recommendation: ask for audience and desired outline before drafting.

best_practices: Run validation before generation, refactoring, or drafting starts.
Keep project standards current so checks reflect the team's real expectations.
Ask one or two specific clarification questions when input is close to valid.
anti_patterns: Using the skill after implementation work is already complete.
Treating vague requirements as valid because they mention a broad topic.
Rejecting near-complete input without suggesting concrete clarification questions.

faq.question: What does this skill validate?
faq.answer: It validates clarity, completeness, format, context, and alignment with saved project standards.

faq.question: Does it generate the final work?
faq.answer: No. It decides whether the input is ready for the next workflow step.

faq.question: What happens when standards are missing?
faq.answer: It falls back to general validation and checks whether the request has enough detail to proceed.

faq.question: Can it ask follow-up questions?
faq.answer: Yes. It should recommend specific clarification questions when requirements are incomplete.

faq.question: Is it useful outside coding tasks?
faq.answer: Yes. It can validate writing briefs, documentation requests, and other structured project inputs.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
